title: Sampling Rate Alteration: Downsampling, Upsampling, and Interpolation
summary: This page introduces sampling-rate alteration for discrete-time signals, defining downsampling as x_d[n] = x[Mn] and explaining that it retains every Mth sample while reducing the sampling rate. It also defines expansion/upsampling by an integer factor L via x_e[n], where zeros are inserted between samples, and motivates interpolation to reconstruct the inserted samples.
